title: Brighton Boat Rental: How to Choose Your Charter
html:
When planning a boat rental in Brighton, one of the most popular departure points is Brighton Marina. Day rates for a small motorboat start from around £250, and you can compare verified listings on BoatHire24 to find the best option for your group.

Where to set off in Brighton

Most charters leave from Brighton Marina, a sheltered harbour with full facilities and easy parking. Other launch points include Shoreham Harbour to the west, Newhaven Marina further east, and Hove Lagoon for smaller dinghies. Beyond the shoreline, popular destinations include Beachy Head, Rottingdean, and the Sow and Pigs Reef.

Which boat to rent

Your choice of vessel depends on group size, preferred activity, and budget. Here is a breakdown of common options available in Brighton.

  1. Motorboat, ideal for a half-day coastal cruise with up to six guests, from £250.
  2. Sailing yacht, great for a relaxing full-day trip with up to eight guests, from £400.
  3. Rib, perfect for a speedy adventure to Beachy Head with up to eight guests, from £350.
  4. Catamaran, spacious for a full-day party with up to twelve guests, from £800.
  5. Luxury yacht, for a premium crewed experience such as the Maiora 26m, sleeping ten, from £1,500. For more details, see this luxury yacht listing on BoatHire24.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Brighton?

Prices vary from £50 per person for a shared tour to over £2,000 for a full-day crewed yacht. Below is a typical price band breakdown.

  • Shared RIB tour: £50, £80 per person, around 1, 2 hours
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours): £250, £400 for up to six guests
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours): £800, £1,200 for up to twelve guests
  • Luxury crewed yacht full-day: £1,500, £3,000 including skipper and fuel

To find the best deals, you can book a charter online and compare prices across multiple boat owners.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Brighton?

No licence is required for most bareboat rentals if you are over 18 and have basic boating experience. In UK waters, a licence is generally not mandatory for recreational use of small boats, but the operator will confirm your competence. For larger or crewed vessels, a skipper is included.

Tip: Book a RIB for a fun inter-trip to Shoreham Harbour and keep an eye on the tide times; low tide exposes the mudflats near Hove Lagoon.

Season for Brighton boat rental

The peak season runs from May to September, when the weather is warm and the sea is calmest. During high summer, south-westerly winds are common, so plan to set off in the morning before the sea breeze strengthens. Spring and autumn offer quieter conditions and lower prices.

At a glance

  • Price band: £50, £2,000+
  • Peak months: May, September
  • Trip length: 1, 8 hours
  • Guest capacity: 2, 12 people
  • Currency: GBP
  • Departure: Brighton Marina

Questions about boat rental in Brighton

Can I rent a boat in Brighton without a licence?

Yes, most hire companies do not require a licence for smaller boats, but they may ask for a brief competency check or offer a skipper.

What is the best time of day for a boat trip in Brighton?

Morning trips are generally calmer due to lighter winds, while afternoons are best for sunbathing and swimming.

How far can I sail from Brighton in a rental boat?

A typical half-day cruise covers up to 10 nautical miles, reaching as far as Beachy Head or Shoreham.

Are there any age restrictions for renting a boat in Brighton?

You must be at least 18 years old to rent a boat; some operators require 21 for larger vessels.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Brighton or list your own. For more options, browse the listings across the UK and beyond. If you own a boat in Brighton, consider listing it on the platform to reach thousands of potential renters in 2026.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Brighton and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
